basic_details.job_title: AECS Hyderabad Teaching Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for TGT, Balvatika Teacher and More Posts
basic_details.application_mode: Offline - form download and submission against Panel-specific link
basic_details.short_information: Atomic Energy Central School-2 (AECS-2), Hyderabad has invited applications from eligible and interested candidates for filling teaching posts purely on contract basis for the Academic Year 2026-27 (01/04/2026 to 31/03/2027) at AEC Schools-1 and 2, DAE Colony, ECIL Post, Hyderabad. The posts include Trained Graduate Teachers (TGT), Librarian, and Balvatika Teachers (BVT), to be filled against leave and regular vacancies. Selection is through a walk-in Skill Test and Practical Classroom Observation (Demo Teaching Class).

age_limit.details: Pay is subject to applicable TDS as per Rule 17. | All age relaxations are concurrent - if a candidate qualifies for more than one, only the highest single relaxation applies.

qualification.details: Three-year Bachelor's/Honours degree with minimum 50% marks (or the four-year integrated degree route with B.Ed from an NCTE-recognized institution) plus a pass in CTET Paper-II. Must have studied English as an Elective/Main subject in at least 3 years/6 semesters of the degree course. Knowledge of Computer Applications is desirable. | Must have studied any two of History, Geography, Economics, and Political Science during graduation - one of which must be History or Geography as a Main subject for at least 3 years/6 semesters, and the second for at least one year/two semesters. Candidates under the Choice Based Credit System (CBCS) with these subjects are also eligible. B.Ed and CTET Paper-II are required as per the general TGT criteria. | Must have studied Hindi/Sanskrit as an Elective/Main subject for at least 3 years/6 semesters of the degree course, along with B.Ed and CTET Paper-II. | Graduation degree with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university/institute. Any one of: B.Ed (Special Education) with 50%+ marks; B.Ed (General) + 1-year or 2-year Diploma in Special Education; B.Ed (General) + Post Graduate Professional Diploma (PGPD) in Special Education; B.Ed Special Education + Post Graduate Professional Certificate (PGPC); PG Diploma in Special Education (Mental Retardation/Multiple Disability/Locomotor Impairment); Secondary Level Teacher Training in Visual Impairment; Senior Diploma in Teaching the Deaf; or BA B.Ed with 50%+ marks in Visual Impairment; or any other RCI-approved equivalent qualification. Qualified in CTET (Elementary Stage) - up to 5% relaxation in qualifying marks for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates. Registration with the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI) with a valid CRR number. Proficiency to teach in Hindi and English. | Bachelor's Degree in Library Science, or Library & Information Science, from a recognized university with minimum 50% marks, plus knowledge of Computer Applications and working knowledge of Hindi and English. | Bachelor's degree in Drawing and Painting/Sculpture/Graphic Art/Fine Arts with minimum 50% marks (Drawing and Painting must be the Main subject throughout the degree), plus a B.Ed degree with minimum 50% marks from an NCTE-recognized institution. Working knowledge of English/Hindi/regional language, school work experience, and computer knowledge are desirable. | Four-year integrated course/Bachelor's Degree in Physical Education, or Bachelor of Physical Education and Sports from an NCTE-recognized institution, with minimum 50% marks - OR graduation plus B.P.Ed with minimum 50% marks separately in each. State-level representation in a recognized sport, working knowledge of English/Hindi, school experience, and a National Institute of Sports diploma are desirable. | Senior Secondary/Higher Secondary or equivalent with minimum 50% aggregate marks, with English as a subject at +2 level. Diploma in Nursery Teachers Education/Pre-School Education/Early Childhood Education Programme (D.E.C.Ed) of at least 2 years, or B.Ed (Nursery) from an NCTE-recognized institution. Proficiency in teaching in English medium. Working knowledge of computers is required; working knowledge of Hindi is desirable. | Note: For TGT posts, candidates without B.Ed but with 60%+ marks (Science subjects) or 55%+ marks (Humanities) in graduation may be considered at the sole discretion of the school, if candidates with the requisite qualification are unavailable.

selection_process: Day and Date Post and Subject
Wednesday, 22/07/2026 Balvatika Teacher (BVT), TGT (English), TGT (Social Science), TGT (Hindi/Sanskrit)
Thursday, 23/07/2026 TGT (Special Educator), TGT (Art), TGT (PET - Male/Female), Librarian

Candidates will be shortlisted and empaneled through a Skill Test and Practical Classroom Observation (Demo Teaching Class) as per the notified schedule. Candidates must come prepared to present both a topic of their own choice and another topic chosen at random from a box on the day of the test.

Reporting time is 8:00 AM to 12:00 Noon on the scheduled test date; candidates will not be entertained beyond reporting hours. Cut-off marks for shortlisting will be based on marks scored in both years of Intermediate/+2 for PRT and BVT posts
and marks across all years of graduation (including languages) for TGT posts. If the number of registered candidates increases
the number of test days may be extended
with updates posted on the AECS-2 notice board.
how_to_apply: Download the application form from: www.nfc.gov.in, www.aecshyd1.edu.in, www.aecshyd2.edu.in, www.amd.gov.in, or www.ecil.co.in.
Fill the application completely with bio-data, qualification details, and correspondence information.
Attach attested photocopies of all relevant mark sheets and certificates.
Submit the application against the appropriate link provided separately for Panel-I, Panel-II, and Panel-III, by 18/07/2026, 5:00 PM. Applications by email will not be entertained.
Submit a separate application for each post if applying for more than one post.
Report for the Skill Test/Demo Class with original documents on the scheduled date (22/07/2026 or 23/07/2026, as applicable) between 8:00 AM and 12:00 Noon.
important_instructions: Proficiency in teaching in English is compulsory for all categories except regional language posts. Candidates applying for contract teacher posts should have experience taking online/offline classes and computer knowledge for conducting online classes and tests. Teachers retired from KVS, NVS, or AEES services are eligible to apply if below 65 years of age, and must appear for the Skill Test and Demo Class. Incomplete applications, or those without required mark sheet photocopies, will be rejected. If applications in any category/subject are high, the eligibility cut-off percentage will be decided at the sole discretion of AEC Schools, Hyderabad. No TA/DA is admissible for attending the Skill Test and Demo Class. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification. No interim correspondence will be entertained regarding shortlisting or empanelment. AEC Schools, Hyderabad reserves the right to amend the number of posts, withdraw advertised posts, or not fill any post at its discretion. In case of discrepancy between Hindi and English content, the English content is final.

content_sections.heading: AECS Hyderabad Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates who are interested in applying for the AECS Hyderabad Recruitment 2026 should first download and carefully read the official notification PDF. The notification contains complete details about vacancy distribution, eligibility criteria, age limit, stipend/salary, selection process, important dates, and application instructions. Reading the official document is important to ensure that you meet all requirements before submitting the application form. Download AECS Hyderabad Job 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: AECS Hyderabad Contract Teacher Recruitment 2026-27: Quick Overview

rows.details: Offline - form download and submission against Panel-specific link

content_sections.heading: Panel Structure
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for Panel-I must have CTET qualification - Paper-I for PRT and Paper-II for TGT.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Since this is a walk-in process, only shortlisted candidates (after verification of original documents) will be called for the Skill Test and Practical Classroom Observation on the scheduled day.

rows.event: Skill Test / Demo Class - BVT, TGT (English, Social Science, Hindi/Sanskrit)
Skill Test / Demo Class - TGT (Special Educator, Art, PET), Librarian

content_sections.heading: Pay and Age Limit
content_sections.text: Pay is subject to applicable TDS as per Rule 17. All age relaxations are concurrent - if a candidate qualifies for more than one, only the highest single relaxation applies.

rows.relaxation: As per Government of India norms

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification (Subject-wise)
content_sections.text: Three-year Bachelor's/Honours degree with minimum 50% marks (or the four-year integrated degree route with B.Ed from an NCTE-recognized institution) plus a pass in CTET Paper-II. Must have studied English as an Elective/Main subject in at least 3 years/6 semesters of the degree course. Knowledge of Computer Applications is desirable. Must have studied any two of History, Geography, Economics, and Political Science during graduation - one of which must be History or Geography as a Main subject for at least 3 years/6 semesters, and the second for at least one year/two semesters. Candidates under the Choice Based Credit System (CBCS) with these subjects are also eligible. B.Ed and CTET Paper-II are required as per the general TGT criteria. Must have studied Hindi/Sanskrit as an Elective/Main subject for at least 3 years/6 semesters of the degree course, along with B.Ed and CTET Paper-II. Graduation degree with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university/institute. Any one of: B.Ed (Special Education) with 50%+ marks; B.Ed (General) + 1-year or 2-year Diploma in Special Education; B.Ed (General) + Post Graduate Professional Diploma (PGPD) in Special Education; B.Ed Special Education + Post Graduate Professional Certificate (PGPC); PG Diploma in Special Education (Mental Retardation/Multiple Disability/Locomotor Impairment); Secondary Level Teacher Training in Visual Impairment; Senior Diploma in Teaching the Deaf; or BA B.Ed with 50%+ marks in Visual Impairment; or any other RCI-approved equivalent qualification. Qualified in CTET (Elementary Stage) - up to 5% relaxation in qualifying marks for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates. Registration with the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI) with a valid CRR number. Proficiency to teach in Hindi and English. Bachelor's Degree in Library Science, or Library & Information Science, from a recognized university with minimum 50% marks, plus knowledge of Computer Applications and working knowledge of Hindi and English. Bachelor's degree in Drawing and Painting/Sculpture/Graphic Art/Fine Arts with minimum 50% marks (Drawing and Painting must be the Main subject throughout the degree), plus a B.Ed degree with minimum 50% marks from an NCTE-recognized institution. Working knowledge of English/Hindi/regional language, school work experience, and computer knowledge are desirable. Four-year integrated course/Bachelor's Degree in Physical Education, or Bachelor of Physical Education and Sports from an NCTE-recognized institution, with minimum 50% marks - OR graduation plus B.P.Ed with minimum 50% marks separately in each. State-level representation in a recognized sport, working knowledge of English/Hindi, school experience, and a National Institute of Sports diploma are desirable. Senior Secondary/Higher Secondary or equivalent with minimum 50% aggregate marks, with English as a subject at +2 level. Diploma in Nursery Teachers Education/Pre-School Education/Early Childhood Education Programme (D.E.C.Ed) of at least 2 yea

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Candidates will be shortlisted and empaneled through a Skill Test and Practical Classroom Observation (Demo Teaching Class) as per the notified schedule. Candidates must come prepared to present both a topic of their own choice and another topic chosen at random from a box on the day of the test. Reporting time is 8:00 AM to 12:00 Noon on the scheduled test date; candidates will not be entertained beyond reporting hours. Cut-off marks for shortlisting will be based on marks scored in both years of Intermediate/+2 for PRT and BVT posts, and marks across all years of graduation (including languages) for TGT posts. If the number of registered candidates increases, the number of test days may be extended, with updates posted on the AECS-2 notice board.

rows.post_and_subject: Balvatika Teacher (BVT), TGT (English), TGT (Social Science), TGT (Hindi/Sanskrit)
TGT (Special Educator), TGT (Art), TGT (PET - Male/Female), Librarian

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Duly filled application form (downloaded from the specified websites) with all relevant photocopies of essential and desirable qualification documents Complete bio-data with age, qualification, experience, and correspondence address, contact number, email ID Passport-size photograph Attested photocopies of mark sheets (Intermediate, Graduation, B.Ed, CTET, etc.) Original documents for verification, to be produced on the day of the Skill Test and Demo Class

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Download the application form from: www.nfc.gov.in , www.aecshyd1.edu.in , www.aecshyd2.edu.in , www.amd.gov.in , or www.ecil.co.in . Fill the application completely with bio-data, qualification details, and correspondence information. Attach attested photocopies of all relevant mark sheets and certificates. Submit the application against the appropriate link provided separately for Panel-I, Panel-II, and Panel-III, by 18/07/2026, 5:00 PM. Applications by email will not be entertained. Submit a separate application for each post if applying for more than one post. Report for the Skill Test/Demo Class with original documents on the scheduled date (22/07/2026 or 23/07/2026, as applicable) between 8:00 AM and 12:00 Noon.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Proficiency in teaching in English is compulsory for all categories except regional language posts. Candidates applying for contract teacher posts should have experience taking online/offline classes and computer knowledge for conducting online classes and tests. Teachers retired from KVS, NVS, or AEES services are eligible to apply if below 65 years of age, and must appear for the Skill Test and Demo Class. Incomplete applications, or those without required mark sheet photocopies, will be rejected. If applications in any category/subject are high, the eligibility cut-off percentage will be decided at the sole discretion of AEC Schools, Hyderabad. No TA/DA is admissible for attending the Skill Test and Demo Class. Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification. No interim correspondence will be entertained regarding shortlisting or empanelment. AEC Schools, Hyderabad reserves the right to amend the number of posts, withdraw advertised posts, or not fill any post at its discretion. In case of discrepancy between Hindi and English content, the English content is final.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for AECS Hyderabad Teaching Staff recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the offline application for AECS Hyderabad Teaching Staff recruitment is 18-07-2026. Ensure your application reaches before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. What are the different teaching posts available in this recruitment?
faq.answer: The available teaching posts include Balvatika Teacher (BVT), Trained Graduate Teacher (TGT) in various subjects like English, Social Science, Hindi/Sanskrit, Special Educator, ART, PET (Male/Female), and Librarian.

faq.question: Q3. What is the minimum qualification required for TGT posts?
faq.answer: For TGT posts, candidates generally need a Bachelor's Degree in the relevant subject with at least 50% marks, along with a B.Ed. degree. Specific requirements vary by subject.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Balvatika Teacher and TGT/Librarian posts?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for Balvatika Teacher is 30 years, and for TGT & Librarian posts, it is 35 years. Age relaxation is applicable as per government norms.

faq.question: Q5. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q6. How will candidates be selected for these teaching positions?
faq.answer: The selection process typically involves a Skill Test and a Practical Classroom Observation (Demo Teaching Class).

faq.question: Q7. Can candidates apply online for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, this recruitment is strictly through the offline application mode. Candidates need to download the form and submit it by post.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary offered for TGT and Librarian posts?
faq.answer: The consolidated salary for TGT and Librarian posts is Rs. 34,125/- per month.

faq.question: Q9. Where can I download the application form?
faq.answer: The application form can be downloaded from the official websites of AECS Hyderabad, such as www.aecshyd2.edu.in, or other mentioned links in the notification.

faq.question: Q10. Is experience required for these teaching positions?
faq.answer: While not always mandatory, experience in a relevant field or working in a school is often considered desirable for some posts, as mentioned in the detailed qualification criteria.
